title: Harpalyce altissima
intro: Harpalyce altissima, also known as the "butterfly pea," is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its climbing habit and vibrant blue-violet flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Harpalyce altissima include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Harpalyce altissima is classified as a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the tribe Phaseoleae, which includes other legumes such as beans and peas. The genus Harpalyce is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Harpalyce altissima is a climbing or twining legume that produces clusters of small, fragrant flowers. The plant is a member of the subfamily Faboideae, which is one of the largest and most diverse subfamilies within the Fabaceae family.
